what_it_does: It frames a design stance for the dragnet-development channel: the project's own fabric (addresses, resolver, typed-ADDRESS edges, mark-is-truth, board runtime, phone surface) should remain the canonical source of truth, while Obsidian is plugged in beneath/beside as a secondary host that contributes its UI, graph, search, and sync affordances.
observations: Title uses the 'Obsidian as HOST, not MASTER' metaphor.
It explicitly states it will NOT converge on a single answer.
It lists specific Obsidian qualities to borrow: graph view, backlinks, instant search, Dataview/Bases, Canvas, plugin ecosystem, iOS app, sync.
It names core project fabric concepts: addresses, the one resolver, typed-ADDRESS edges, mark-is-truth, board runtime, phone surface.
It claims the discussion is grounded in actual files rather than generic Obsidian praise.
The visible content ends after the introductory section; the promised architectures and mechanical law are not present in the provided file.
standouts: The 'host not master' framing and the explicit refusal to pick one architecture up front.
The enumerated list of Obsidian affordances the project wants to leverage.
The mismatch between the stated scope and the truncated body.
conventions: Follows the noticeboard item front-matter schema with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, timestamps, and history.
Uses a markdown heading plus preamble consistent with a design note.
Breaks completeness expectation by promising candidate architectures and a mechanical law that do not appear in the visible content.
concerns: The file appears truncated relative to its stated scope.
It claims to lay out candidate architectures and state a mechanical law, but only the framing paragraph is present.
It relies on project-specific jargon without defining those terms inside this file.
notes: A design-note preamble arguing the project fabric should own truth while Obsidian provides a secondary lens, though the promised architectures are missing from the visible file.
questions: What is the exact mechanical law that operationalizes 'host not master'?
What are the candidate architectures and their concrete trade-offs?
Which actual files ground the discussion?
How would synchronization and conflict resolution work between the fabric and Obsidian?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to present the 'host not master' law and compare grounded candidate architectures; the actual visible content only establishes the framing and lists Obsidian affordances, so it partially matches but lacks the promised architectures.
apparent_themes: Canonical source of truth versus presentation host.
Avoiding tool lock-in while leveraging mature third-party capabilities.
Separation of data model from user interface.
Grounded design reasoning over generic tool praise.
intention_signals: It is trying to establish a governing architectural principle that prevents Obsidian from becoming the authoritative system of record, and to prepare the ground for comparing concrete integration patterns.
novelty: The explicit 'host not master' metaphor and the enumerated checklist of Obsidian strengths to be borrowed without ceding canonical ownership.
contribution: It contributes a clear framing principle for integration (project fabric owns truth; Obsidian is a host/lens) and a checklist of Obsidian affordances worth preserving, which can guide later integration design.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard design note in the dragnet-development channel arguing that the project's address/resolver/board fabric should remain the canonical source of truth while Obsidian is used as a secondary host for graph, search, and mobile UI affordances.
intention_for_embedding: To establish the 'host not master' principle and set up a grounded comparison of integration architectures between the project fabric and Obsidian.
